title: Soundtrack to Instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: Soundtrack to Instrumentality             Quote Post by SaVaGe » Thu Aug 16, 2012 12:22 am I am bored and this is a stupid thread. If its stupidity is too much for these forums, or if it has been done before, feel free to close it. But if you could choose a song/songs to be the "soundtrack" to your, er, "individual" experience during human instrumentality, what would it/they be? :3 I'm still thinking of my own..

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Thu Aug 16, 2012 12:41 am Does it have to be a song? When I think of myself turning into an orange puddle, I can only imagine the voice of the Kool-Aid guy going OH YEAH!

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Thu Aug 16, 2012 12:55 am Just about anything by Placebo would work for me.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Thu Aug 16, 2012 1:22 am Hmm, can think of only a couple at the moment: Radiohead - Lucky Sigur Rós - Untitled #4 AKA Njósnavélin I'm really brain dead ATM though, so I will probably come back with my definitive answer later.

[]: Seriously. BD             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Aug 16, 2012 3:17 am Why the hell would you need anything other than this ? It fits the context perfectly .

[]:             Quote Post by Justacrazyguy » Thu Aug 16, 2012 8:18 am Cromathishe Phantasie(I don´t know if this is the correct name)

[]: Re: Seriously. BD             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Aug 16, 2012 8:53 am Alaska Slim wrote: Why the hell would you need anything other than this ? It fits the context perfectly .

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Thu Aug 16, 2012 2:51 pm Flash Delirium by MGMT

[]: Re: Seriously. BD             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Thu Aug 16, 2012 3:52 pm Bagheera wrote: Riiiiiight. Not sure what I'd pick. I'm not that tight with music so lots of things not rap, metal, or country could work depending on my mood. Maybe Johnny Cash's cover of Hurt?

[]: Re: Seriously. BD             Quote Post by CJD » Thu Aug 16, 2012 7:10 pm Bagheera wrote: Maybe Johnny Cash's cover of Hurt?

[]: Re: Seriously. BD             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Aug 16, 2012 7:23 pm CJD wrote: That'd... actually work pretty well.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Aug 16, 2012 7:36 pm "Only" by Nine Inch Nails "Come Together" by The Beatles (lol) "Where Is My Mind" by Pixies "My Way" by Frank Sinatra (because of the person I'd probably see as my transition guide) Anything from Daft Punk's "Discovery," I guess. "Clair de Lune" by Debussy "II Air" by Bach "Suiten fur Violoncello solo N. 1" by Bach Kind of an ecclectic selection, I know. That's what I could come up with off the top of my head.

[]:             Quote Post by Tankred » Thu Aug 16, 2012 7:44 pm This is how I'll roll out.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Thu Aug 16, 2012 9:09 pm Tankred wrote: This is how I'll roll out.

[]:             Quote Post by aboose » Thu Aug 16, 2012 9:55 pm For some reason Instrumentality reminds me of Planetes, so naturally I thought of something from that soundtrack. プラネテス　[PLANETES]

[]:             Quote Post by SaVaGe » Thu Aug 16, 2012 10:03 pm Johnny Cash's rendition of Hurt would be a rather gloomy way to get tanged.. riffraff11235 wrote: "Come Together" by The Beatles (lol)

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Fri Aug 17, 2012 12:56 am https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7FDAkpQSJVA

[]: Re: Seriously. BD             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Aug 17, 2012 2:17 am Bagheera wrote: Riiiiiight.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Fri Aug 17, 2012 3:46 am A Pink Floyd song would fit nicely, like The Great Gig in the Sky . But I am more a Alan Parsons Project fan and their instrumentals would also do nicely also. But the top two choices for me would have to be either the overture from A Bridge too Far or Gimme Shelter by The Rolling Stones .

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Fri Aug 17, 2012 5:52 am SaVaGe wrote: Johnny Cash's rendition of Hurt would be a rather gloomy way to get tanged..
